title: 10 best herbal creams for jet lag

remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Valerian cream's sedative and relaxant properties can help promote a good night's sleep, allowing your body to adjust to the new time zone and reduce symptoms of jet lag.

Muscle Relaxation and Soothing Properties: The cream's muscle relaxation and soothing properties can help alleviate any physical discomfort or stiffness that may have developed during long periods of travel, making it easier to unwind and relax upon arrival.

Adaptogenic and Stress-Reducing Properties: Valerian's adaptogenic and stress-reducing properties can help regulate your body's natural rhythms and reduce feelings of anxiety, fatigue, and disorientation often experienced when traveling across time zones.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with jet lag because it promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality. The active compounds in valerian root, such as isovaleric acid and valerenic acid, interact with the brain's GABA receptors to reduce stress and anxiety. By calming the nervous system, valerian creams can help regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate symptoms of jet lag, including fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. This makes it easier to adjust to new time zones and get back on a normal sleep schedule.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid is a sesquiterpene that acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can be beneficial in alleviating symptoms of jet lag.
Valeranone: Valeranone is a valerenic acid derivative that has sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce the effects of jet lag.

Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid is a short-chain fatty acid that has been shown to have sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which can help mitigate the sleep disturbances and fatigue associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are the most commonly used part for making creams to alleviate jet lag due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which promote relaxation and reduce stress.
Flowers: The flowers of Valeriana officinalis are used in creams for jet lag as they contain valeranone, a compound that helps to regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep.
Seeds: The seeds of Valeriana officinalis are used in creams for jet lag as they contain valerenic acids and other compounds that help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Extract the dried roots of valeriana officinalis using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a 1:2 ratio.
Steep the extracted mixture in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear extract.
Mix the extract with a carrier oil like coconut oil in a 20% concentration for a smooth consistency.
Whip the mixture into a creamy texture using a hand mixer or blender for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lemon balm cream can help alleviate anxiety and stress caused by time zone changes, promoting a restful night's sleep.
Calming effects on the nervous system: The herb's calming effects can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia, irritability, and fatigue.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm cream may also help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by travel-related changes in diet and environment, which can exacerbate jet lag symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with jet lag because they contain natural compounds that promote relaxation and reduce stress. The herb's calming effects can help regulate the body's internal clock, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, the creams may also help alleviate symptoms of fatigue and insomnia associated with jet lag, promoting a restful night's sleep and a smoother transition to a new time zone. This can lead to improved mood and reduced travel fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by jet lag, promoting a smoother transition to a new time zone.

Luteolin: A flavonoid that has been shown to regulate the body's circadian rhythms, luteolin can help adjust the body's internal clock to the new time zone, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and insomnia.

Geraniol: A terpene with a calming effect, geraniol can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel and time zone changes, promoting a more restful sleep and faster adaptation to the new time zone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are used to make creams for jet lag due to their calming and sedative properties, which help regulate sleep patterns.
Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis are used in creams for jet lag due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to time zone changes and reduce stress.
Roots: The roots of Melissa officinalis are used in creams for jet lag due to their ability to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, helping to alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of melissa officinalis flowers and leaves from a clean and dry area at dawn.
Combine the harvested melissa officinalis with 2 cups of distilled water in a stainless steel pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to a simmer for 20 minutes to release the essential oils.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter with the melissa officinalis infusion to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and sleep patterns.
Melatonin-rich herbs: Contain natural melatonin, which helps regulate sleep-wake cycles and can help alleviate insomnia caused by jet lag.
Adaptogenic properties of Ashwagandha: Help the body adapt to stress and fatigue associated with travel, allowing for a smoother transition to new time zones and reducing jet lag symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with jet lag because it contains a unique combination of natural ingredients that promote relaxation and regulate the body's internal clock. The calming properties of Passiflora incarnata help to reduce anxiety and stress associated with travel, while its adaptogenic properties assist in adjusting to new time zones. By promoting a restful sleep and reducing fatigue, these creams enable travelers to recover faster from the effects of jet lag, making them a popular choice for those who frequently cross time zones.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane is a beta-carboline alkaloid found in Passiflora incarnata. It has been shown to have sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate the stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.

Methyl 6-methylsalicylate: This phenolic compound has been found in Passiflora incarnata and has been reported to have sedative and relaxing properties. It can help regulate the body's internal clock and promote better sleep, which is essential for recovering from jet lag.

Isovitexin: Isovitexin is a flavonoid glycoside found in Passiflora incarnata. It has been reported to have sedative and anxiolytic properties, as well as antioxidant activity, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in flavonoids, which have been found to have sedative and sleep-promoting effects, making them a common ingredient in jet lag creams.
Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are known to contain a high concentration of passifloric acid, which has been shown to have a sedative effect and help regulate sleep patterns.
Seeds: The seeds of Passiflora incarnata are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making them a popular ingredient in jet lag creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper bags at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and mix it with beeswax and a preservative to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Figwort cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and discomfort caused by travel-related fatigue, making it easier for the body to adjust to new time zones.

Adaptogenic property: Figwort is an adaptogen, which means it helps the body adapt to stressors like jet lag by balancing the body's natural cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, allowing for a smoother transition into the new timezone.
Antioxidant property: Figwort cream's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, reducing fatigue, and promoting overall well-being.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scrophularia nodosa, also known as figwort, creams helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and reduce inflammation caused by disrupted sleep patterns. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and improve sleep quality. Additionally, figwort creams may help alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag, including fatigue, headaches, and digestive issues. By using these creams, individuals can help their bodies adapt to new time zones and feel more rested and refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help regulate the body's circadian rhythms by influencing the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN), which is responsible for synchronizing the body's internal clock with the external environment.
Flavonoids (especially rutin): Flavonoids like rutin may help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by disrupted circadian rhythms.

Triterpenes (especially ursolic acid): Triterpenes like ursolic acid may help regulate the body's internal clock by influencing the expression of genes involved in circadian rhythm regulation, thus potentially aiding in the recovery from jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their reported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Used because they are believed to have antiviral and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Used for their potential anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried scrophularia nodosa roots from the plant in late summer or early autumn.
Infuse the dried roots in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 45c for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 hours.
Mix 30g of beeswax with 30ml of the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60c for 10 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and store it in the refrigerator for 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile creams help reduce inflammation caused by jet lag, which can lead to pain and discomfort in the body.
Sedative properties: The sedative compounds in chamomile creams promote relaxation, calming the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep after a long flight.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile creams' antioxidants help combat oxidative stress caused by travel, which can disrupt the body's natural rhythms and make it harder to adjust to new time zones.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with jet lag because of its calming and soothing properties. The active ingredients in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and promote relaxation. By using chamomile cream, individuals can reduce stress and anxiety associated with jet lag, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. This can lead to improved sleep quality, reduced fatigue, and a faster recovery from travel-related fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and regulating sleep patterns due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties.

Alpha-bisabolol: Alpha-bisabolol, a sesquiterpene alcohol, helps with jet lag by soothing the skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation, which may aid in readjusting the body's circadian rhythms.

Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, helps with jet lag by reducing oxidative stress, promoting the production of melatonin (the sleep hormone), and regulating the body's circadian rhythms to help adjust to new time zones.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Their calming and soothing properties help reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, they contribute to the anti-inflammatory and soothing effects in jet lag creams.
Seeds: Containing apigenin, a flavonoid with sleep-promoting properties, they help regulate sleep patterns.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to discard the solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of the infused matricaria chamomilla liquid to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to regulate circadian rhythms, helping to adjust to new sleep-wake cycles after travel across time zones.

Hypotensive effect: The active compounds in St John's Wort cream, such as hypericin and flavonoids, have a relaxing effect on blood vessels, which can help alleviate jet lag-related fatigue and promote relaxation.
Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort has antioxidant activity that helps to reduce oxidative stress caused by travel, such as inflammation, fatigue, and irritability.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with jet lag because of its adaptogenic properties. It aids in regulating the body's internal clock and promoting a balance between melatonin production, which is crucial for managing sleep patterns. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, St John's Wort creams can help alleviate the fatigue associated with jet lag. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to counteract the oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, allowing the body to recover faster.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This lipophilic constituent may help with jet lag by improving sleep quality due to its ability to modulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce stress levels.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with antioxidant properties, quercetin may help mitigate the effects of jet lag by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with long-distance travel and time zone changes.

Naringenin: A flavanone with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, naringenin may aid in reducing the severity of jet lag symptoms by protecting against the negative impacts of travel on the body's natural sleep-wake cycle.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their bioflavonoid and flavonoid content, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve sleep quality.
Flowers: They are used for their adaptogenic properties, which help the body to adapt to time zone changes and reduce stress.
Seeds: They are used due to their high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that helps to regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep patterns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers at full bloom and store them in a cool dark place.
Grind 20 grams of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 2 grams of the ground flowers with 5 grams of the beeswax mixture and stir until well combined for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing effects: Lavender's calming properties can help reduce stress, anxiety, and insomnia caused by jet lag, promoting a good night's sleep.
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm the body, reducing fatigue and muscle tension associated with jet lag.
Stress-reducing and adaptogenic effects: Lavender's adaptogenic properties can help regulate the body's natural rhythms and reduce stress, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with jet lag because of its calming and relaxing properties. The fragrance and active compounds in these creams can help regulate the body's internal clock, promoting a peaceful sleep environment. This, in turn, can aid in readjusting the body's natural rhythms after travel across time zones. Additionally, lavender's soothing effects can also help alleviate stress and anxiety associated with jet lag, making it easier to adapt to new schedules and environments.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps to regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress levels, which can be disrupted by jet lag. Its sedative properties promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene also has sedative and calming effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and restlessness. Its ability to regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety makes it an effective ingredient in Lavandula angustifolia creams.

Luteolin: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag on the body. Its ability to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce oxidative stress can aid in the recovery from jet lag.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming properties, which help alleviate stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.
Leaves: They are used for their sedative effects, which can aid in improving sleep quality and reducing fatigue caused by jet lag.
Seeds: They are used due to their potential to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation, helping to alleviate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried lavandula flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 1/2 cup of the infused liquid with 1/4 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycerin.
Blend the infused liquid mixture with the melted beeswax and oil mixture until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Oats' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as avenanthramides, help to reduce stress and inflammation caused by travel fatigue, promoting relaxation and rest.

Calming and Sedative Effects: The presence of herbs like lavender, chamomile, and valerian in oats creams can induce a calming effect on the mind and body, helping to alleviate jet lag-related anxiety and insomnia.

Hydrating and Moisturizing Properties: Oats' emollient properties help to lock in moisture, soothing dry skin and eyes often affected by air travel, and providing relief from fatigue-induced dehydration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Avena sativa, also known as oats, creams helps with jet lag because of its unique properties that promote relaxation and reduce stress. The cream's active compounds, including avenanthramides, have been shown to have a calming effect on the body, helping to regulate the body's internal clock. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, Avena sativa creams can help alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. This makes it an effective natural remedy for travelers.
remedy_constituents: Avenacosides: Avenacosides, a type of saponin, may help with jet lag by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag, such as fatigue and stress.

Avenanthramides: Avenanthramides, a unique type of phenolic compound found in oats, may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to jet lag symptoms such as fatigue and circadian rhythm disruption.

Avenantharidin: Avenantharidin, a compound found in Avena sativa, may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that could help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag on the body, such as fatigue, sleep disturbances, and digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are rich in saponins, which have a sedative effect on the body and help alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag.
Roots: The roots contain a wealth of nutrients and antioxidants that promote relaxation and help regulate the body's internal clock.
Leaves: The leaves of Avena sativa are high in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce stress and promote a restful sleep, essential for recovering from jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa root and leaves in large quantities preferably in the morning when they are at their highest potency.
Dry the harvested avena sativa root and leaves in a warm environment at 40 degrees celsius for two weeks.
Grind the dried avena sativa root and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 3000 rpm for five minutes.
Mix the ground avena sativa powder with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Add 10 milliliters of distilled water and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the cooled mixture and stir well.

remedy_properties: Melatonin: Some herbal creams contain melatonin, a hormone that regulates sleep-wake cycles. Melatonin supplements can help the body adjust to new time zones and improve sleep quality.
Valerian root: Valerian root has been traditionally used as a natural sedative and relaxant, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep despite jet lag.
Ginkgo biloba: Ginkgo biloba is believed to improve blood flow to the brain, which may help alleviate symptoms of fatigue, headaches, and dizziness commonly associated with jet lag.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with jet lag because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that improve blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function and memory. The antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba cream also help to reduce oxidative stress caused by travel fatigue. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as headaches and fatigue, allowing the body to recover faster and adjust to the new time zone. This results in a smoother transition and reduced jet lag symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel-related fatigue and disrupted sleep patterns. By reducing inflammation and promoting antioxidant defenses, flavonoids may help alleviate jet lag symptoms.

Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone may help regulate the body's internal clock by influencing the expression of genes involved in circadian rhythm regulation, which can be disrupted by travel across time zones.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid and potent antioxidant, quercetin may help mitigate the negative effects of travel stress, such as fatigue, headaches, and decreased cognitive function, by reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Barks: They contain ginkgolides, which are thought to improve circulation and help the body adjust to new time zones.
Seeds: They contain bilobalide, a compound believed to aid in the reduction of jet lag symptoms and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 15-20 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees at dawn to capture optimal oil content and store them in a cool place.
Extract the oil from the leaves using a cold press method or solvent extraction with a 1:5 ratio for 8 hours.
Mix the extracted oil with 5 grams of beeswax and 2 grams of lanolin in a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Add 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 5 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and blend for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow the herbal ginkgo biloba creams to cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Digestive Soothing Properties: Peppermint's calming effects on the digestive system can help ease stomach discomfort, nausea, and bloating caused by jet lag, allowing for a more restful sleep.

Pain Relieving Properties: Peppermint's analgesic properties can help alleviate headaches, migraines, and muscle tension often experienced during travel and time zone changes, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Stomach Regulating Properties: Peppermint's ability to regulate digestion and reduce symptoms like bloating, gas, and indigestion can help adjust the body to new food sources and schedules, making it easier to transition into a new time zone.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with jet lag because it promotes a sense of refreshment and revitalization. The menthol in peppermint creams stimulates the brain and helps to increase alertness, making it easier to overcome the fatigue associated with jet lag. Additionally, the cooling sensation of peppermint creams can help to reduce stress and promote a sense of calm, allowing the body to adjust to its new time zone more efficiently. This can lead to a smoother and more enjoyable travel experience.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps alleviate jet lag by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, promoting better sleep and relaxation.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool has a sedative effect and helps regulate sleep patterns, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Menthol: This terpene helps improve circulation, which can aid in reducing fatigue and discomfort associated with jet lag, and its refreshing properties can also help stimulate the body and mind.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain menthol, which helps to reduce fatigue and relieve headaches associated with jet lag.
Leaves: They also contain menthone, which has a calming effect and helps to regulate sleep patterns.
Leaves: As a natural anti-inflammatory, peppermint leaves can help to reduce swelling and relieve body aches caused by traveling.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of fresh menthapiperita leaves and 1 cup of distilled water for the infusion.
Steam distill the menthapiperita leaves to obtain 1 ounce of essential oil in 2 hours.
Combine 1 ounce of menthapiperita essential oil with 8 ounces of a carrier oil like sweet almond oil.
Mix the oil and carrier oil mixture with 16 ounces of a water and emulsifier base in 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 10 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime creams helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to regulate the body's natural rhythms. The cream's calming properties help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel, while its adaptogenic ingredients help to normalize the body's internal clock. This allows travelers to adjust to their new time zone more easily, minimizing the symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tilia cordata cream has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by jet lag, promoting relaxation and reducing fatigue.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The herb contains flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects, helping to alleviate the symptoms of insomnia and restlessness associated with jet lag.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tilia cordata cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and pain caused by jet lag-related fatigue, making it easier to recover from travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help regulate circadian rhythms and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Buds: High in essential oils and resins, providing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to soothe skin irritation caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers and leaves in a clean environment free of contaminants.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 6 hours or air drying for 10 days.
Combine 200g of dried tilia platyphyllos with 400ml of organic coconut oil and 100ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth and consistent.
Strain the cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, allowing it to cool before storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with jet lag because they contain a unique combination of herbs and essential oils that help regulate the body's internal clock. The citrus and bitter notes in these creams stimulate the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that regulates sleep-wake cycles. As the body adjusts to new time zones, the bitter orange cream provides a gentle push towards natural sleep patterns, reducing the discomfort and fatigue associated with jet lag. This allows travelers to adapt more easily to their new environment and arrive feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal bitter orange creams contain adaptogenic compounds like flavonoids and limonoids, which help the body adapt to new environments and rhythms, reducing stress and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal bitter orange creams, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by travel-related stressors like changes in diet, environment, and sleep patterns.

Melatonin-boosting compounds: Some herbal bitter orange creams contain melatonin-boosting compounds like valerian root and hops, which can help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms and promote a restful night's sleep, even during times of disruption caused by jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain flavonoids and coumarins that help regulate sleep-wake cycles, making them effective in treating jet lag-related insomnia.
Seeds: Bitter orange seeds contain sesquiterpenes that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality citrus x aurantium peel and dry it in a warm oven at 50°c for 4 hours.
Mix dried peel with 100g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax, stirring until the mixture is smooth.
Add 5g of vitamin e oil and 2g of lavender essential oil to the mixture, blending well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes, whipping constantly until it thickens.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at 3°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with jet lag because they promote natural hormone balance, which is disrupted by crossing time zones. The cream's active ingredient, vitex agnus-castus, stimulates the production of hormones that regulate sleep-wake cycles, helping to adjust to new schedules and reduce fatigue. Additionally, chaste tree creams can alleviate stress and anxiety caused by travel, further aiding in a smooth transition back to your regular routine. By supporting hormonal balance, chaste tree creams can help you feel refreshed and revitalized despite the challenges of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Hormonal Balancing Properties: Chaste tree cream helps regulate hormonal imbalances caused by jet lag, which can disrupt sleep patterns and overall well-being.

Adaptogenic Properties: The cream's adaptogenic effects enable the body to cope better with stress and fatigue associated with travel across time zones, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of jet lag.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Chaste tree extract has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to fall asleep despite disruptions caused by jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chaste tree roots are rich in essential oils that help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: The barks of the chaste tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate fatigue and stress associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried vitex agnus castus leaves and flowers from a reliable source in quantities of approximately 20 grams.
Combine the harvested herb with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for one hour to facilitate the infusion process without excessive heat damage.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the herb residue from the extracted oil, discarding solids and retaining 100 milliliters of oil.
Mix the strained oil with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler until fully melted, then pour into glass containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with jet lag because they contain adaptogenic herbs that help regulate body rhythms. The calming properties of hawthorn soothe the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. Additionally, hawthorn's adaptogenic effects allow it to normalize cortisol levels, which can become disrupted due to changes in sleep patterns. By using a hawthorn cream, individuals can promote relaxation, reduce fatigue, and ease the transition back into their regular routine after crossing time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Hawthorn's adaptogenic properties help the body adjust to changes in environment, stress, and rhythm, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and disorientation.

Cardio-protective compounds: Hawthorn's cardio-protective compounds, such as flavonoids and procyanidins, may help regulate blood flow and circulation, which can reduce symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and anxiety associated with jet lag.

Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel-induced inflammation and stress, which can contribute to fatigue, headaches, and other symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm the skin after long flights.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling caused by jet lag-related fatigue.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, which nourish and regenerate the skin while reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems from local pharmacies or online suppliers.
Combine the dried herb with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a dark glass jar and store for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 20g of beeswax pellets with 40ml of coconut oil and 30ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Add the crataegus monogyna-infused olive oil to the melted wax mixture and stir until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with jet lag because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that promotes liver health and detoxification. When we travel across time zones, our bodies struggle to adjust to new sleep-wake cycles, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. Silymarin in milk thistle creams supports the body's natural rhythms by gently stimulating the liver's natural detox processes, helping to eliminate excess stress hormones and toxins that can disrupt our circadian rhythms. This natural cream can help alleviate jet lag symptoms and promote a smoother transition to our new time zone.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by time zone changes, promoting faster adaptation to new environments.

Silibinin: Silibinin, another bioactive compound present in milk thistle, exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, headaches, and joint pain often experienced during jet lag.

Cardiotonic properties: Milk thistle's cardiotonic properties can help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms by supporting the heart's normal functioning and promoting a sense of calmness, reducing symptoms like insomnia and anxiety commonly associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a potent antioxidant that helps protect against liver damage caused by jet lag-related fatigue and stress.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as headaches and insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum plants and dry them properly in a warm well-ventilated area for at least 7 days.
Combine 20g of dried silybum marianum flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the herbal extract into the carrier oil then let it cool down completely.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the filtered liquid.
Whip 100g of beeswax with 200ml of the infused oil until smooth and creamy adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil for a calming effect.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with jet lag because they contain natural ingredients that promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and regulate cortisol levels. The soothing properties of dandelion can help ease stress and anxiety associated with traveling across time zones. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate jet lag symptoms such as fatigue, headaches, and joint pain. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal dandelion creams can help your body adjust to its new environment more quickly, reducing the discomfort of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Dandelion's adaptogenic properties help regulate the body's response to stress, including the stress caused by jet lag, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.
Digestive soothing effects: The anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds in dandelion cream can calm digestive issues that often accompany jet lag, such as nausea, bloating, and cramps.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel fatigue, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing inulin, which helps regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of jet lag-related gastrointestinal distress.
Barks: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe skin irritation caused by jet lag-induced fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried off for freshness and potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 3-4 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for optimal extraction.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered taraxacum officinale with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler on low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a glass jar, discarding any solids and letting it cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with jet lag because they contain menthol, which has a natural ability to ease fatigue and promote relaxation. When used topically on the skin, menthol can help stimulate blood flow and reduce muscle tension, making it easier for the body to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, wintergreen's calming properties can soothe any irritability or stress associated with traveling, allowing you to more easily adapt to a new sleep schedule. As a result, herbal wintergreen creams can provide a natural solution for alleviating jet lag symptoms and promoting a smoother travel experience.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help to stimulate blood flow and reduce feelings of fatigue and grogginess often associated with jet lag.
Glycosides: Wintergreen contains glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.

Essential Oils: The essential oils present in wintergreen, such as methyl salicylate and camphor, have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to promote relaxation and reduce stress associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots have been traditionally used to treat fatigue and stress, making them a popular choice for medicinal creams aimed at alleviating the symptoms of jet lag.

Barks: The bark of the wintergreen plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, both of which are beneficial for combating jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and dry them in a warm dark place for at least seven days.
Combine two tablespoons of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves with one cup of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in the refrigerator for 14 hours to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Whip one cup of shea butter with two tablespoons of the reserved infused oil until smooth and creamy for use as a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with jet lag because of their unique ability to regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle. The calming properties of hops work to soothe an overactive mind, allowing for a deeper and more restful sleep. Additionally, hops' sedative effects help to counteract the stimulating effects of caffeine and other substances that can disrupt sleep patterns. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, herbal hop creams can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and wake up feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Herbal hops creams contain adaptogens like humulone, which help the body adapt to stressors like travel disruptions, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of anxiety and fatigue.

Sedative and calming effects: The sedative compounds in herbal hops creams, such as linalool, can help regulate sleep patterns and induce a sense of calm, making it easier to fall asleep despite jet lag.

Anxiolytic properties: Herbal hops creams contain anxiolytic compounds like xanthohumol, which can reduce anxiety levels and promote relaxation, helping individuals cope with the stress and discomfort associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have a calming effect on the body and mind, promoting relaxation and reducing fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves and flowers, drying them for at least four hours in the shade.
Combine 20 grams of dried humulus lupulus with 40 grams of coconut oil, heating mixture over low heat for one hour.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and stir until fully incorporated, allowing the mixture to cool completely overnight.
Mix in 5 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil, whipping the cream until smooth and creamy.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars, storing them in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi creams helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, calmness, and clarity of mind. The herb Brahmi, also known as Bacopa monnieri, has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to enhance cognitive function, memory, and mental well-being. When applied topically, it can help regulate the body's internal clock, reducing stress and anxiety caused by time zone changes. As a result, brahmi creams can help improve sleep quality, reduce fatigue, and increase energy levels, making them an effective natural remedy for jet lag relief.
remedy_properties: Cerebroprotective properties: Brahmi's ability to protect the brain from damage helps alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and mental fogginess.
Anxiolytic properties: Brahmi's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by time changes, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep schedule.
Memory-enhancing properties: Brahmi's ability to improve memory and cognitive function can aid in the process of adjusting to a new environment and reducing the disorienting effects of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Brahmi roots have been traditionally used to calm the mind and promote relaxation, making them an excellent choice for jet lag relief.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of brahmi contain a high concentration of antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that help regulate sleep patterns and reduce fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried bacopa monnieri leaves from a trusted supplier in a ratio of 10:1.
Grind the bacopa monnieri leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered bacopa monnieri with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 60°c to infuse the oil with the herbal extract without exceeding 10 minutes.
Strain and filter the cream through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into sterilized containers within 10 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jet lag are topical preparations containing natural extracts that help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The best herbal creams for jet lag include Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Silybum marianum, Humulus lupulus, and Hypericum perforatum. If left untreated, jet lag can disrupt daily routines, affect work and social life, and even lead to irritability, mood swings, and decreased productivity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal creams are typically related to circadian rhythm disruptions or sleep disorders. Herbal creams containing ingredients like valerian root, chamomile, and lavender may help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, restlessness, and fatigue often experienced during times of travel or seasonal changes. These natural remedies can promote relaxation, reduce stress, and regulate the body's internal clock to improve overall sleep quality.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with jet lag is a blend of Peppermint, Lavender, and Chamomile. Peppermint helps to ease fatigue and mental fogginess, while Lavender promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system. Chamomile soothes the skin and reduces inflammation, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. Applying a cream containing these herbs can also improve sleep quality and reduce jet lag symptoms, allowing you to feel refreshed and revitalized after a long flight. This combination can be applied topically to the skin or inhaled for optimal benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and reduce stress caused by time zone changes. The valerian root, a natural ingredient in these creams, has a calming effect on the body, easing symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and fatigue. By regulating sleep patterns and promoting a sense of calm, Valeriana officinalis creams can help travelers adapt to new time zones, making them a useful tool for managing the effects of jet lag and improving overall travel well-being.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This constituent helps to regulate sleep patterns by inhibiting the activity of GABA receptors, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced jet lag symptoms.
Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid has a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which are essential in managing jet lag symptoms such as fatigue and insomnia.
Valeranone: Valeranone exhibits anxiolytic properties, reducing anxiety and stress levels, which is particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing jet lag-induced anxiety and restlessness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root is commonly used due to its high valerenic acid content, which promotes relaxation and sleep quality, helping alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are another part used, as they contain a combination of alkaloids that support the plant's calming effects.
Roots: Valerian root is also used for its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adjust to changing time zones and environmental factors associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with a brush and rinsing with distilled water for 10 minutes.
Infuse 100g of the cleaned roots in 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and mix it with 100g of shea butter and 100g of coconut oil.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes until it thickens and becomes a smooth, spreadable cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with jet lag because they contain the herb lemon balm, known for its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The calming properties of Melissa officinalis can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and insomnia, by regulating the body's internal clock. Additionally, the herb's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress caused by travel-related changes in environment and routine, promoting a smoother transition for the body.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that helps with jet lag by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by disrupted circadian rhythms.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that aids in combating jet lag by regulating the body's internal clock and improving sleep quality through its influence on the suprachiasmatic nucleus.

Volatile oils (especially linalool and linalyl acetate): A blend of terpenes that contributes to alleviating jet lag symptoms by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving sleep quality through their sedative and anxiolytic properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their calming and stress-relieving properties.
Flowers: The flowers are often used due to their fragrance and flavor, and they contain a higher concentration of essential oils compared to the leaves.
Stems: They are sometimes used as a source of essential oils and for their medicinal properties, although less commonly than leaves and flowers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of melissa officinalis flowers and leaves, dried in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Combine 1 cup of dried melissa officinalis with 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to the infused oil, heating the mixture gently over low heat until the beeswax dissolves.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with jet lag because they contain the calming properties of lavender oil, which can regulate the body's internal clock and promote relaxation. The sedative effects of lavender can aid in falling asleep and improve the quality of sleep, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. Additionally, the calming scent of lavender can reduce stress and anxiety associated with traveling, allowing the body to adapt to the new environment more efficiently. This can lead to a smoother transition and reduced jet lag symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving sleep quality through its sedative and anxiolytic properties.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to alleviating jet lag symptoms by reducing anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep patterns due to its calming and sedative effects.
Limonene: May help with jet lag by reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and improving mood through its antidepressant and anxiolytic properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain calming properties that help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Flowers: Rich in calming oils and fragrances, they promote relaxation and improve sleep quality to counteract jet lag effects.
Buds: Containing high concentrations of calming essential oils, they help reduce anxiety and stress associated with traveling across time zones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steep 1/4 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Combine the cooled herbal infusion with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a saucepan.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 10-15 minutes until it thickens into a smooth cream texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with jet lag because they contain the extract of Passiflora incarnata, also known as purple passionflower. This herb has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are common symptoms of jet lag. By calming the mind and body, Passiflora incarnata creams can help individuals adjust to new time zones more easily. Its sedative properties also support a restful night's sleep, making it an effective tool for managing jet lag and its associated symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: This alkaloid helps with jet lag by regulating the body's internal clock and sleep-wake cycle, which can be disrupted by time zone changes.
Isophorone: This isophorone has sedative properties that help individuals adjust to new sleep schedules and reduce the symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia and fatigue.
Rotundine: This alkaloid has a sedative effect and is believed to help with jet lag by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of passifloric acid, which has sedative and relaxing properties that help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Fruits: They are used because they are a rich source of vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin C, potassium, and antioxidants, which help reduce fatigue and promote overall well-being.
Barks: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and help regulate sleep patterns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves to use in the recipe immediately.
Steep the herb mixture in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to infuse the liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with 1 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes while stirring occasionally until it thickens into a gel.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and whip it with 1 tablespoon of beeswax until it thickens into a creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with jet lag because they contain the extract of oats, which is known for its calming and relaxing properties. This natural ingredient can help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce the symptoms associated with jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. By promoting a restful night's sleep and soothing the body and mind, Avena sativa creams can help individuals adjust to a new time zone and feel more refreshed and revitalized. This makes it an ideal solution for travelers.
remedy_constituents: Avenacosides: These saponin glycosides help regulate the body's circadian rhythm and reduce stress caused by jet lag, promoting a sense of relaxation and normal sleep-wake cycles.

Fatty alcohols: These compounds, particularly 1-octacosanol and 1-tetracosanol, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to jet lag symptoms such as fatigue and muscle soreness.

Phenolic acids: These antioxidants, including ferulic and vanillic acids, help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin affected by jet lag.
Leaves: They are a good source of flavonoids, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, alleviating the symptoms of jet lag.
Roots: They contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help calm and soothe the skin, reducing jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried avena sativa roots and set them aside for further use.
Combine 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 10g of shea butter to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Add 20g of distilled water and 5g of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and pour it into 250ml glass jars for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with jet lag because they promote improved blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function and reducing fatigue. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba help to combat oxidative stress caused by traveling across time zones. By increasing oxygen delivery to the brain, Ginkgo biloba creams can aid in regulating sleep patterns and circadian rhythms. This, in turn, helps to alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia and disorientation, allowing travelers to adjust to their new environment more efficiently.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help mitigate the effects of jet lag by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with time zone changes, promoting better adaptation to new environments.

Terpene lactones (specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolides): These compounds aid in improving cognitive function, memory, and mood, which are often disrupted by jet lag, allowing individuals to recover faster from travel-related fatigue.

Phenolic acids (specifically, quercetin and isorhapontigenin): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as headaches, fatigue, and digestive issues, by reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used in creams for jet lag due to their high flavonoid content, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve sleep quality.
Seeds: The seeds are used in creams for jet lag as they contain ginkgolides, which have been shown to have a sedative effect and promote better sleep.
Leaves: The leaves are also used in creams for jet lag due to their antioxidant properties, which help to protect against oxidative stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine ginkgo biloba extract 5% 1 gram with beeswax 2 grams in a double boiler.
Add jojoba oil 10 grams and sweet almond oil 10 grams to the mixture in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes until the beeswax melts completely.
Remove the double boiler from the heat source and let it cool for 30 minutes.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass container and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with jet lag because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has been shown to alleviate symptoms of fatigue and discomfort associated with air travel. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce muscle soreness and joint pain, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, ginger has been found to stimulate digestion, which can help to regulate the body's internal clock and improve overall sleep quality. This can aid in faster recovery from jet lag.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help alleviate jet lag symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
Shogaols: Shogaols help regulate the body's circadian rhythms by increasing the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that helps control sleep-wake cycles.
Volatile oils (mainly Gingerols and Shogaols): These oils help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with jet lag, making it easier to travel and adjust to new environments.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help to alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag, such as nausea and fatigue.
Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and improving circulation, making them a popular choice for jet lag creams.

Leaves: Although less commonly used than the rhyzomes and roots, the leaves of Zingiber officinale contain flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 pound of dried zingiber officinale root in 4 cups of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the infused oil and melt in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and reaches a creamy texture within 5 minutes.
Pour the herbal cream into sterile jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with jet lag because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that assists the body in adapting to time zone changes. By promoting liver function and reducing oxidative stress, silymarin supports the body's natural rhythm, helping to mitigate the effects of jet lag. Additionally, Silybum marianum creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate fatigue and discomfort often associated with traveling across time zones. This natural remedy can aid in a smoother transition to the new time zone.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with jet lag by acting as antioxidants and free radical scavengers, which can mitigate oxidative stress caused by travel-induced time zone changes.
Silymarin: This mixture of flavonoids helps with jet lag by protecting the body against cell damage and inflammation, reducing the negative effects of time zone changes on the body.
Linoleic acid: This fatty acid helps with jet lag by reducing inflammation and improving the integrity of the skin's natural barrier, which can become compromised during travel and time zone changes.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of silymarin, a polyphenol that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids, which are believed to help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them suitable for jet lag relief.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their saponin content, which is thought to have calming and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the early morning when the plant is dry and free of dew.
Dry the harvested silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves with 500 milliliters of jojoba oil in a heat-proof container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 4 to 6 hours or until it reaches a temperature of 100 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass container and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus creams helps with jet lag because they contain the herb hop, which has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The sedative properties of Humulus lupulus can help regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. By reducing fatigue and promoting a restful night's sleep, Humulus lupulus creams can help alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, making it an effective natural remedy for travelers.
remedy_constituents: Lupulone: This sesquiterpene compound has been shown to have sedative properties, which can help alleviate the insomnia and disrupted sleep patterns often associated with jet lag.
Humulone: Another sesquiterpene compound, humulone has been found to have a calming effect, reducing anxiety and stress related to traveling across time zones.

Xanthohumol: A prenylated chalcone, xanthohumol has been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their sedative properties, which help alleviate insomnia and promote relaxation, a common symptom of jet lag.
Leaves: Their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties make them useful in soothing skin irritation and promoting a restful night's sleep after traveling.
Buds: They contain compounds with a calming effect, which may help reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, promoting a more balanced sleep-wake cycle.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10g of dried humulus lupulus flowers in the morning when they are free of moisture.
Combine the dried flowers with 30g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat at 60c for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 5g of beeswax to the coconut oil mixture and heat at 80c for 1 hour to emulsify.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2 hours before applying to the skin as a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with jet lag because they contain hyperforin, a compound that regulates the body's circadian rhythms. By applying the cream to the skin, it is absorbed and helps to synchronize the body's internal clock with the new time zone. This can reduce symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. Additionally, the cream's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate any skin irritation caused by travel. This natural remedy can provide relief and support during travel.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Helps with jet lag by regulating the body's internal clock and reducing the symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.
Naphthodianthrones (including Hypericin): Contributes to alleviating jet lag symptoms by modulating the body's circadian rhythms and improving the quality of sleep.
Flavonoids (including Quercetin and Kaempferol): Assists in mitigating the effects of jet lag by acting as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting overall well-being during travel.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to help reduce jet lag symptoms.
Flowers: They are rich in hyperforin and hypericin, which may help regulate sleep patterns and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may aid in reducing stress and promoting relaxation, helping to alleviate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried up.
Dry the harvested hypericum perforatum in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10-15 minutes and then remove from heat to let it cool and solidify.
